description.en: Closes an open synchronous file handle, disabling any further operations on it and releasing the exclusive lock previously put on the file associated with the file handle.

excerpt.es: Closes an open synchronous file handle, disabling any further operations on it and releasing the exclusive lock previously put on the file associated with the file handle.
excerpt.en: Closes an open synchronous file handle, disabling any further operations on it and releasing the exclusive lock previously put on the file associated with the file handle.

description.en: Returns aPromisewhich resolves to aFileSystemSyncAccessHandleobject that can be used to synchronously read from and write to a file. The synchronous nature of this method brings performance advantages, but it is only usable inside dedicatedWeb Workers.

excerpt.es: Returns aPromisewhich resolves to aFileSystemSyncAccessHandleobject that can be used to synchronously read from and write to a file. The synchronous nature of this method brings performance advantages...
excerpt.en: Returns aPromisewhich resolves to aFileSystemSyncAccessHandleobject that can be used to synchronously read from and write to a file. The synchronous nature of this method brings performance advantages...

description.en: Returns aPromisewhich resolves to a newly createdFileSystemWritableFileStreamobject that can be used to write to a file.

excerpt.es: Returns aPromisewhich resolves to a newly createdFileSystemWritableFileStreamobject that can be used to write to a file.
excerpt.en: Returns aPromisewhich resolves to a newly createdFileSystemWritableFileStreamobject that can be used to write to a file.

description.en: A reference to the file system handle that the change was observed on.For the user-observable file system, this can be aFileSystemFileHandleor aFileSystemDirectoryHandle.For theOrigin Private File System(OPFS), it can be aFileSystemFileHandle, aFileSystemDirectoryHandle, or aFileSystemSyncAccessHandle.This property will benullfor records with a"disappeared","errored", or"unknown"type.
